revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sawi-seret]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

[[Bild:Aquarius constellation map.png|thumb|Karte des Sternbildes Wassermann]]
'''Sawi-seret''' (''Flanke des Schafs'') ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte.
Der auffälligste Sterm ist hierbei [[Sadalsuud|Sadalsuud (β)]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] wurde Tepi-a Sopdet in der Sethos-Schrift als 19. Dekan geführt; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] erschien Tepi-a Sopdet an 18. Stelle.
== Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]]
== Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 91. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62 und S. 396.
[[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flanke des Schafs'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte. Die Ableitung der Übersetzung ''Flanke'' entstammt der Form ''s3wt'' und ist auf den Körperteil zwischen Steißbein und Rücken bezogen, weshalb in diesem Zusammenhang von ''flankierend'' und ''schützend'' gesprochen wird. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Sadalsuud|Sadalsuud (β)]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]]. In den Dekanlisten am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] wurde Sawi-seret in der Sethos-Schrift als 19. Dekan geführt; in der Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] erschien Sawi-seret an 18. Stelle. == Siehe auch == * [[Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flanke des Schafs'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Die Ableitung der Übersetzung ''Flanke'' entstammt der Form ''s3wt'' und ist auf den Körperteil zwischen Steißbein und Rücken bezogen, weshalb in diesem Zusammenhang von ''flankierend'' und ''schützend'' gesprochen wird. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Sadalsuud|Sadalsuud (β)]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Sawi-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 20. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|16. Achet III]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. Aufgrund der [[Extinktion (Astronomie)|Extinktion]] ist Sawi-seret bis zu einer Horizonthöhe von 2,9° sichtbar. Der scheinbare Auf- oder Untergang erfolgt gegenüber dem tatsächlichen Auf- oder Untergang etwa 18 bis 21 Minuten später beziehungsweise früher. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flanke des Schafs'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung für einen [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekan]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, das in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und z.B. auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Die Ableitung der Übersetzung ''Flanke'' stammt von der Form ''s3wt'' und bezieht sich auf den Körperteil zwischen Steißbein und Rücken, weshalb in diesem Zusammenhang auch von ''flankierend'' und ''schützend'' gesprochen wird. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hier [[Sadalsuud|Sadalsuud (β)]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Sawi-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 20. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|16. Achet III]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. Aufgrund der [[Extinktion (Astronomie)|Extinktion]] ist Sawi-seret bis zu einer Horizonthöhe von 2,9° sichtbar. Der scheinbare Auf- oder Untergang erfolgt etwa 18 bis 21 Minuten später bzw. früher als der tatsächliche Auf- oder Untergang. == Siehe auch == * [[Sawi-qed-seret]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 91. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flanke des Schafs'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung für einen [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekan]], der vier [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] gehörte, das in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und z. B. auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Die Ableitung der Übersetzung ''Flanke'' stammt von der Form ''s3wt'' und bezieht sich auf den Körperteil zwischen Steißbein und Rücken, weshalb in diesem Zusammenhang auch von ''flankierend'' und ''schützend'' gesprochen wird. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hier [[Sadalsuud|Sadalsuud (β)]] im Sternbild [[Wassermann (Sternbild)|Wassermann]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Sawi-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 20. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Achet#Datierungen|16. Achet III]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. Aufgrund der [[Extinktion (Astronomie)|Extinktion]] ist Sawi-seret bis zu einer Horizonthöhe von 2,9° sichtbar. Der scheinbare Auf- oder Untergang erfolgt etwa 18 bis 21 Minuten später bzw. früher als der tatsächliche Auf- oder Untergang. == Siehe auch == * [[Sawi-qed-seret]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 91. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:サウィ・セレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Sawi-seret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:サウィ・セレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sawi-seret]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[en:draft:Sawi-seret]] [[de:Sawi-seret]][[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sawi-seret]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sawi-seret]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sawi-seret]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sawi-seret]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Sawi-seret]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{illChristian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 Eqq N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. ''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret}} == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|12=draft|12=draft}} == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)|qq=draft}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|qq=draft}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|qq=draft}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star|qq=}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarius (constellation)|qq=}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh year of reign. Due to {{ill|Extinction (astronomy)|de|Extinktion (Astronomie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extinction (astronomy)|qq=}}, Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Star|qq=}} in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation {{ill|Aquarius (constellation)|de|Wassermann (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aquarius (constellation)|qq=}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh year of reign. ''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = V17-G43-M17 E11 N14-N33:N33:N33*N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-seret
''S3wj-sr.t''
''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Sawi-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sawi-seret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sawi-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sawi-seret]] }} [[sn:Sawi-seret]] [[ja:サウィ・セレト]] [[de:Sawi-seret]] [[sn:awi-seret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. ''Flank of the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-seret''' is the designation for an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised four {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and belonged to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The derivation of the translation ''flank'' originates from the form ''s3wt,'' referring to the body part between the tailbone and back, and in this context is also associated with "flanking" and "protecting." The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=16th Achet III|de|16. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Due to [[:en:Extinction (astronomy)|Extinction (astronomy)]], Sawi-seret is visible up to a horizon altitude of 2.9°. The apparent rising or setting occurs approximately 18 to 21 minutes later or earlier than the actual rising or setting. == See also == * {{ill|Sawi-qed-seret|de|Sawi-qed-seret|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawi-qed-seret|qq=}} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 91. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Sawi-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:サウィ・セレト]] [[de:Sawi-seret]] {{wikidata link|Q2228296}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] in this decan is {{ill|Sadalsuud|de|Sadalsuud (β)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sadalsuud|qq=}} in the constellation [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-seret represented the 20th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. Achet III|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign.
